3	 *
4	 * **Example:** Instantiate pipeline using the `pipeline` function.
5	 * ```javascript
6	 * import { pipeline } from '@huggingface/transformers';
7	 *
8	 * const classifier = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis');
9	 * const output = await classifier('I love transformers!');
10	 * // [{'label': 'POSITIVE', 'score': 0.999817686}]
11	 * ```

13	 * @module pipelines

80	/**
81	 * @typedef {string | RawImage | URL | Blob | HTMLCanvasElement | OffscreenCanvas} ImageInput
82	 * @typedef {ImageInput|ImageInput[]} ImagePipelineInputs
83	 */
84	
85	/**
86	 * Prepare images for further tasks.
87	 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} images images to prepare.
88	 * @returns {Promise<RawImage[]>} returns processed images.
89	 * @private
90	 */
91	async function prepareImages(images) {
92	    if (!Array.isArray(images)) {
93	        images = [images];
94	    }
95	
96	    // Possibly convert any non-images to images
97	    return await Promise.all(images.map(x => RawImage.read(x)));
98	}

100	/**
101	 * @typedef {string | URL | Float32Array | Float64Array} AudioInput
102	 * @typedef {AudioInput|AudioInput[]} AudioPipelineInputs
103	 */
104	
105	/**
106	 * Prepare audios for further tasks.
107	 * @param {AudioPipelineInputs} audios audios to prepare.
108	 * @param {number} sampling_rate sampling rate of the audios.
109	 * @returns {Promise<Float32Array[]>} The preprocessed audio data.
110	 * @private
111	 */
112	async function prepareAudios(audios, sampling_rate) {
113	    if (!Array.isArray(audios)) {
114	        audios = [audios];
115	    }
116	
117	    return await Promise.all(audios.map(x => {
118	        if (typeof x === 'string' || x instanceof URL) {
119	            return read_audio(x, sampling_rate);
120	        } else if (x instanceof Float64Array) {
121	            return new Float32Array(x);
122	        }
123	        return x;
124	    }));
125	}

127	/**
128	 * @typedef {Object} BoundingBox
129	 * @property {number} xmin The minimum x coordinate of the bounding box.
130	 * @property {number} ymin The minimum y coordinate of the bounding box.
131	 * @property {number} xmax The maximum x coordinate of the bounding box.
132	 * @property {number} ymax The maximum y coordinate of the bounding box.
133	 */
134	
135	/**
136	 * Helper function to convert list [xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax] into object { "xmin": xmin, ... }
137	 * @param {number[]} box The bounding box as a list.
138	 * @param {boolean} asInteger Whether to cast to integers.
139	 * @returns {BoundingBox} The bounding box as an object.
140	 * @private
141	 */
142	function get_bounding_box(box, asInteger) {
143	    if (asInteger) {
144	        box = box.map(x => x | 0);
145	    }
146	    const [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] = box;
147	
148	    return { xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax };
149	}

160	/**
161	 * The Pipeline class is the class from which all pipelines inherit.
162	 * Refer to this class for methods shared across different pipelines.
163	 */
164	export class Pipeline extends Callable {
165	    /**
166	     * Create a new Pipeline.
167	     * @param {Object} options An object containing the following properties:
168	     * @param {string} [options.task] The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
169	     * @param {PreTrainedModel} [options.model] The model used by the pipeline.
170	     * @param {PreTrainedTokenizer} [options.tokenizer=null] The tokenizer used by the pipeline (if any).
171	     * @param {Processor} [options.processor=null] The processor used by the pipeline (if any).
172	     */
173	    constructor({ task, model, tokenizer = null, processor = null }) {
174	        super();
175	        this.task = task;
176	        this.model = model;
177	        this.tokenizer = tokenizer;
178	        this.processor = processor;
179	    }
180	
181	    /** @type {DisposeType} */
182	    async dispose() {
183	        await this.model.dispose();
184	    }
185	}

1975	export class ImageToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
1976	
1977	    /**
1978	     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.
1979	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
1980	     */
1981	    constructor(options) {
1982	        super(options);
1983	    }
1984	
1985	    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
1986	    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {
1987	
1988	        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
1989	        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
1990	
1991	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);
1992	
1993	        const toReturn = [];
1994	        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
1995	            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
1996	            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
1997	            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
1998	                skip_special_tokens: true,
1999	            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
2000	            toReturn.push(decoded);
2001	        }
2002	
2003	        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
2004	    }
2005	}

2732	export class DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
2733	
2734	    /**
2735	     * Create a new DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline.
2736	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
2737	     */
2738	    constructor(options) {
2739	        super(options);
2740	    }
2741	
2742	    /** @type {DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback} */
2743	    async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {}) {
2744	
2745	        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1
2746	
2747	        // Preprocess image
2748	        const preparedImage = (await prepareImages(image))[0];
2749	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImage);
2750	
2751	        // Run tokenization
2752	        const task_prompt = `<s_docvqa><s_question>${question}</s_question><s_answer>`;
2753	        const decoder_input_ids = this.tokenizer(task_prompt, {
2754	            add_special_tokens: false,
2755	            padding: true,
2756	            truncation: true,
2757	        }).input_ids;
2758	
2759	        // Run model
2760	        const output = await this.model.generate({
2761	            inputs: pixel_values,
2762	            // @ts-expect-error TS2339
2763	            max_length: this.model.config.decoder.max_position_embeddings,
2764	            decoder_input_ids,
2765	            ...generate_kwargs,
2766	        });
2767	
2768	        // Decode output
2769	        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output))[0];
2770	
2771	        // Parse answer
2772	        const match = decoded.match(/<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/);
2773	        let answer = null;
2774	        if (match && match.length >= 2) {
2775	            answer = match[1].trim();
2776	        }
2777	        return [{ answer }];
2778	    }
2779	}

1978	     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.
1979	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
1980	     */
1981	    constructor(options) {
1982	        super(options);
1983	    }
1984	
1985	    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
1986	    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {
1987	
1988	        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
1989	        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
1990	
1991	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);
1992	
1993	        const toReturn = [];
1994	        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
1995	            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
1996	            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
1997	            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
1998	                skip_special_tokens: true,
1999	            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
2000	            toReturn.push(decoded);
2001	        }
2002	
2003	        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
2004	    }
2005	}

103	 */

105	/**

107	 * @param {AudioPipelineInputs} audios audios to prepare.
108	 * @param {number} sampling_rate sampling rate of the audios.
109	 * @returns {Promise<Float32Array[]>} The preprocessed audio data.
110	 * @private
111	 */
112	async function prepareAudios(audios, sampling_rate) {
113	    if (!Array.isArray(audios)) {
114	        audios = [audios];
115	    }
116	
117	    return await Promise.all(audios.map(x => {
118	        if (typeof x === 'string' || x instanceof URL) {
119	            return read_audio(x, sampling_rate);
120	        } else if (x instanceof Float64Array) {
121	            return new Float32Array(x);
122	        }
123	        return x;
124	    }));

137	 * @param {number[]} box The bounding box as a list.
138	 * @param {boolean} asInteger Whether to cast to integers.
139	 * @returns {BoundingBox} The bounding box as an object.
140	 * @private
141	 */
142	function get_bounding_box(box, asInteger) {
143	    if (asInteger) {
144	        box = box.map(x => x | 0);
145	    }
146	    const [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] = box;
147	
148	    return { xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax };

input.description: Read test file and pipeline exports
input.prompt:
I need to read specific test infrastructure files in the Transformers.js codebase for planning purposes.

Please:

1. List all files in `tests/pipelines/` directory
2. Read the full content of `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_to_text.js` (the closest existing test to what we're building)
3. Read `tests/unit/pipelines.spec.js` OR `tests/pipelines.spec.js` or whatever the main pipeline test runner file is - look for a file that imports all other pipeline tests
4. Read `tests/init.js` to understand test constants (MAX_MODEL_LOAD_TIME, etc.)
5. Check if there's an `AllTasks` type definition in pipelines.js - run `grep -n "AllTasks" src/pipelines.js`
6. Run `grep -n "PipelineType\|TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs" src/pipelines.js | head -40` to understand all the typedef patterns

Return all content with exact line numbers.

content.text:
I need to read specific test infrastructure files in the Transformers.js codebase for planning purposes.

Please:

1. List all files in `tests/pipelines/` directory
2. Read the full content of `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_to_text.js` (the closest existing test to what we're building)
3. Read `tests/unit/pipelines.spec.js` OR `tests/pipelines.spec.js` or whatever the main pipeline test runner file is - look for a file that imports all other pipeline tests
4. Read `tests/init.js` to understand test constants (MAX_MODEL_LOAD_TIME, etc.)
5. Check if there's an `AllTasks` type definition in pipelines.js - run `grep -n "AllTasks" src/pipelines.js`
6. Run `grep -n "PipelineType\|TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs" src/pipelines.js | head -40` to understand all the typedef patterns

Return all content with exact line numbers.

3414	/**
3415	 * Utility factory method to build a `Pipeline` object.
3416	 *
3417	 * @template {PipelineType} T The type of pipeline to return.
3418	 * @param {T} task The task defining which pipeline will be returned. Currently accepted tasks are:
3419	 *  - `"audio-classification"`: will return a `AudioClassificationPipeline`.
3420	 *  - `"automatic-speech-recognition"`: will return a `AutomaticSpeechRecognitionPipeline`.
3421	 *  - `"depth-estimation"`: will return a `DepthEstimationPipeline`.
3422	 *  - `"document-question-answering"`: will return a `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`.
3423	 *  - `"feature-extraction"`: will return a `FeatureExtractionPipeline`.
3424	 *  - `"fill-mask"`: will return a `FillMaskPipeline`.
3425	 *  - `"image-classification"`: will return a `ImageClassificationPipeline`.
3426	 *  - `"image-segmentation"`: will return a `ImageSegmentationPipeline`.
3427	 *  - `"image-to-text"`: will return a `ImageToTextPipeline`.
3428	 *  - `"object-detection"`: will return a `ObjectDetectionPipeline`.
3429	 *  - `"question-answering"`: will return a `QuestionAnsweringPipeline`.
3430	 *  - `"summarization"`: will return a `SummarizationPipeline`.
3431	 *  - `"text2text-generation"`: will return a `Text2TextGenerationPipeline`.
3432	 *  - `"text-classification"` (alias "sentiment-analysis" available): will return a `TextClassificationPipeline`.
3433	 *  - `"text-generation"`: will return a `TextGenerationPipeline`.
3434	 *  - `"token-classification"` (alias "ner" available): will return a `TokenClassificationPipeline`.
3435	 *  - `"translation"`: will return a `TranslationPipeline`.
3436	 *  - `"translation_xx_to_yy"`: will return a `TranslationPipeline`.
3437	 *  - `"zero-shot-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotClassificationPipeline`.
3438	 *  - `"zero-shot-audio-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotAudioClassificationPipeline`.
3439	 *  - `"zero-shot-image-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotImageClassificationPipeline`.
3440	 *  - `"zero-shot-object-detection"`: will return a `ZeroShotObjectDetectionPipeline`.
3441	 * @param {string} [model=null] The name of the pre-trained model to use. If not specified, the default model for the task will be used.
3442	 * @param {import('./utils/hub.js').PretrainedModelOptions} [options] Optional parameters for the pipeline.

3445	 */

2	 * @file Pipelines provide a high-level, easy to use, API for running machine learning models.
3	 *
4	 * **Example:** Instantiate pipeline using the `pipeline` function.
5	 * ```javascript
6	 * import { pipeline } from '@huggingface/transformers';
7	 *
8	 * const classifier = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis');
9	 * const output = await classifier('I love transformers!');
10	 * // [{'label': 'POSITIVE', 'score': 0.999817686}]
11	 * ```
12	 *
13	 * @module pipelines

  1943	/**
  1944	 * @typedef {Object} ImageToTextSingle
  1945	 * @property {string} generated_text The generated text.

  1947	 *
  1948	 * @callback ImageToTextPipelineCallback Assign labels to the image(s) passed as inputs.
  1949	 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} texts The images to be captioned.
  1950	 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments to pass along to the generate method of the model.
  1951	 * @returns {Promise<ImageToTextOutput|ImageToTextOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the generated text(s).
  1952	 *
  1953	 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageToTextPipelineType
  1954	 */
  1955	
  1956	/**
  1957	 * Image To Text pipeline using a `AutoModelForVision2Seq`. This pipeline predicts a caption for a given image.
  1958	 *
  1959	 * **Example:** Generate a caption for an image w/ `Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning`.
  1960	 * ```javascript
  1961	 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning');
  1962	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
  1963	 * const output = await captioner(url);
  1964	 * // [{ generated_text: 'a cat laying on a couch with another cat' }]
  1965	 * ```
  1966	 *
  1967	 * **Example:** Optical Character Recognition (OCR) w/ `Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten`.
  1968	 * ```javascript
  1969	 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten');
  1970	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/handwriting.jpg';
  1971	 * const output = await captioner(url);
  1972	 * // [{ generated_text: 'Mr. Brown commented icily.' }]
  1973	 * ```
  1974	 */
  1975	export class ImageToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
  1976	
  1977	    /**
  1978	     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.
  1979	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
  1980	     */
  1981	    constructor(options) {
  1982	        super(options);
  1983	    }
  1984	
  1985	    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
  1986	    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {
  1987	
  1988	        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
  1989	        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
  1990	
  1991	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);
  1992	
  1993	        const toReturn = [];
  1994	        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
  1995	            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
  1996	            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
  1997	            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
  1998	                skip_special_tokens: true,
  1999	            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
  2000	            toReturn.push(decoded);
  2001	        }
  2002	
  2003	        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
  2004	    }
  2005	}

  1943	/**

  1949	 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} texts The images to be captioned.
  1950	 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments to pass along to the generate method of the model.
  1951	 * @returns {Promise<ImageToTextOutput|ImageToTextOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the generated text(s).
  1952	 *
  1953	 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageToTextPipelineType
  1954	 */
  1955	
  1956	/**
  1957	 * Image To Text pipeline using a `AutoModelForVision2Seq`. This pipeline predicts a caption for a given image.
  1958	 *
  1959	 * **Example:** Generate a caption for an image w/ `Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning`.
  1960	 * ```javascript
  1961	 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning');
  1962	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
  1963	 * const output = await captioner(url);
  1964	 * // [{ generated_text: 'a cat laying on a couch with another cat' }]
  1965	 * ```
  1966	 *
  1967	 * **Example:** Optical Character Recognition (OCR) w/ `Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten`.
  1968	 * ```javascript
  1969	 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten');
  1970	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/handwriting.jpg';
  1971	 * const output = await captioner(url);
  1972	 * // [{ generated_text: 'Mr. Brown commented icily.' }]
  1973	 * ```
  1974	 */
  1975	export class ImageToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
  1976	
  1977	    /**
  1978	     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.
  1979	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
  1980	     */
  1981	    constructor(options) {
  1982	        super(options);
  1983	    }
  1984	
  1985	    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
  1986	    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {
  1987	
  1988	        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
  1989	        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
  1990	
  1991	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);
  1992	
  1993	        const toReturn = [];
  1994	        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
  1995	            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
  1996	            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
  1997	            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
  1998	                skip_special_tokens: true,
  1999	            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
  2000	            toReturn.push(decoded);
  2001	        }
  2002	
  2003	        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
  2004	    }
  2005	}

1952	 *
1953	 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageToTextPipelineType
1954	 */
1955	
1956	/**
1957	 * Image To Text pipeline using a `AutoModelForVision2Seq`. This pipeline predicts a caption for a given image.
1958	 *
1959	 * **Example:** Generate a caption for an image w/ `Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning`.
1960	 * ```javascript
1961	 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning');
1962	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
1963	 * const output = await captioner(url);
1964	 * // [{ generated_text: 'a cat laying on a couch with another cat' }]
1965	 * ```
1966	 *
1967	 * **Example:** Optical Character Recognition (OCR) w/ `Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten`.
1968	 * ```javascript
1969	 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten');
1970	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/handwriting.jpg';
1971	 * const output = await captioner(url);
1972	 * // [{ generated_text: 'Mr. Brown commented icily.' }]
1973	 * ```
1974	 */
1975	export class ImageToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
1976	
1977	    /**
1978	     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.
1979	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
1980	     */
1981	    constructor(options) {
1982	        super(options);
1983	    }
1984	
1985	    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
1986	    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {
1987	
1988	        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
1989	        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
1990	
1991	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);
1992	
1993	        const toReturn = [];
1994	        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
1995	            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
1996	            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
1997	            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
1998	                skip_special_tokens: true,
1999	            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
2000	            toReturn.push(decoded);
2001	        }
2002	
2003	        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
2004	    }
2005	}
2006

2008	 * @typedef {Object} ImageClassificationSingle
2009	 * @property {string} label The label identified by the model.
2010	 * @property {number} score The score attributed by the model for that label.
2011	 * @typedef {ImageClassificationSingle[]} ImageClassificationOutput
2012	 *

2014	 * @property {number} [top_k=1] The number of top labels that will be returned by the pipeline.
2015	 *

2017	 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} images The input images(s) to be classified.
2018	 * @param {ImageClassificationPipelineOptions} [options] The options to use for image classification.
2019	 * @returns {Promise<ImageClassificationOutput|ImageClassificationOutput[]>} An array or object containing the predicted labels and scores.

* Utility factory method to build a `Pipeline` object.
 *
 * @template {PipelineType} T The type of pipeline to return.
 * @param {T} task The task defining which pipeline will be returned. Currently accepted tasks are:
 *  - `"audio-classification"`: will return a `AudioClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"automatic-speech-recognition"`: will return a `AutomaticSpeechRecognitionPipeline`.
 *  - `"depth-estimation"`: will return a `DepthEstimationPipeline`.
 *  - `"document-question-answering"`: will return a `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`.
 *  - `"feature-extraction"`: will return a `FeatureExtractionPipeline`.
 *  - `"fill-mask"`: will return a `FillMaskPipeline`.
 *  - `"image-classification"`: will return a `ImageClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"image-segmentation"`: will return a `ImageSegmentationPipeline`.
 *  - `"image-to-text"`: will return a `ImageToTextPipeline`.
 *  - `"object-detection"`: will return a `ObjectDetectionPipeline`.
 *  - `"question-answering"`: will return a `QuestionAnsweringPipeline`.
 *  - `"summarization"`: will return a `SummarizationPipeline`.
 *  - `"text2text-generation"`: will return a `Text2TextGenerationPipeline`.
 *  - `"text-classification"` (alias "sentiment-analysis" available): will return a `TextClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"text-generation"`: will return a `TextGenerationPipeline`.
 *  - `"token-classification"` (alias "ner" available): will return a `TokenClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"translation"`: will return a `TranslationPipeline`.
 *  - `"translation_xx_to_yy"`: will return a `TranslationPipeline`.
 *  - `"zero-shot-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"zero-shot-audio-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotAudioClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"zero-shot-image-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotImageClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"zero-shot-object-detection"`: will return a `ZeroShotObjectDetectionPipeline`.
 * @param {string} [model=null] The name of the pre-trained model to use. If not specified, the default model for the task will be used.
 * @param {import('./utils/hub.js').PretrainedModelOptions} [options] Optional parameters for the pipeline.
 * @returns {Promise<AllTasks[T]>} A Pipeline object for the specified task.
 * @throws {Error} If an unsupported pipeline is requested.
 */
export async function pipeline(
    task,
    model = null,
    {
        progress_callback = null,
        config = null,
        cache_dir = null,
        local_files_only = false,
        revision = 'main',
        device = null,
        dtype = null,
        subfolder = 'onnx',
        use_external_data_format = null,
        model_file_name = null,
        session_options = {},
    } = {}
) {
    // Helper method to construct pipeline

    // Apply aliases
    // @ts-ignore
    task = TASK_ALIASES[task] ?? task;

    // Get pipeline info
    const pipelineInfo = SUPPORTED_TASKS[task.split('_', 1)[0]];
    if (!pipelineInfo) {
        throw Error(`Unsupported pipeline: ${task}. Must be one of [${Object.keys(SUPPORTED_TASKS)}]`)
    }
